title: Located, Not Forced current claim-and-premise ledger

premises.name: complex_carrier_realization
premises.statement: Computations use the 128-dimensional complex realization of the 256-dimensional real Cl(9,5) module.

premises.name: computed_carrier_faithfulness
premises.statement: The explicit scripts, not Lean, supply the 192-dimensional carrier and its Hom-space census.

premises.name: lorentzian_transfer_boundary
premises.statement: The bounded physical-signature audit shows that one Lorentzian Hodge half remains complex dimension 192 but is Krein-null and conjugation-exchanged; the stable closure is dimension 384 with signature (192,192). Selection on the true Y14 bundle remains reconstruction-grade.

premises.name: encoded_finite_grammar
premises.statement: The complete formal theorem quantifies over the encoded finite class C_fin, not unrestricted operator theory.

premises.name: codomain_separation
premises.statement: Finite torsion, integer equality/divisibility, dimensions, and diagnostics remain in distinct codomains.

premises.name: framing_convention_packet
premises.statement: The framed-class value depends on the displayed orientation, stabilization, p1/2, and Adams conventions.

premises.name: gu_tangential_identification
premises.statement: Identifying GU's Lambda^2_+ twist with the natural tangential framing is reconstruction-grade.

premises.name: true_y14_source_action_open
premises.statement: The true-Y14 source action, pushforward, and integer-by-construction generation index remain unbuilt.

premises.name: internal_evidence_ceiling
premises.statement: All current replication is internal to the same AI-directed process and is not external validation.

claims.statement: Cl(9,5)=M(64,H); the irreducible real module has real dimension 256 and the computations use its 128-dimensional complex realization.
claims.codomain: Clifford algebra type and real/complex dimensions
claims.grade: standard classification plus internally reproduced construction

claims.statement: The compact/complexified carrier contains a complex 192-dimensional triplet with two 96-dimensional total-chirality blocks. A physical Lorentzian Hodge half preserves those dimensions but is Krein-null and conjugation-exchanged; the computed stable closure has dimension 384 and signature (192,192).
claims.grade: compact carrier computed; bounded Lorentzian fork computed; true-Y14 selection reconstruction-grade

claims.status_anchor: `C_fin` constraints do not supply an odd-prime congruence or select an odd integer
claims.statement: For C_fin, finite torsion rows are 2-primary and integer rows contain only stated equality or power-of-two divisibility information; none supplies a mod-3 congruence or selects a particular odd integer.
claims.codomain: typed finite torsion plus Z-valued constraints
claims.grade: computed carrier premise plus Lean-verified bounded deduction

claims.statement: The standard CRT decomposition applies only after an explicit torsion-valued map into Z/24.
claims.codomain: Z/24 isomorphic to Z/8 x Z/3

claims.statement: The finite Krein transversality result gives zero intersection difference under its finite-dimensional hypotheses.

claims.status_anchor: Antilinear K-null image subspaces retain zero intersection index
claims.statement: The tested K-null antilinear image-subspace class retains zero finite intersection difference.
Under the displayed tangential-framing conventions, the exhibited positive-orientation representative has e_R=+1/12; the opposite orientation has -1/12.
scope_exits: GU-native derivation of the framing identification

claims.status_anchor: The framed class is homotopy-fixed and does not define an integer count
claims.statement: The full framed class has order 12 and its nonzero 3-primary projection has order 3; no additive homomorphism from Z/3 to Z turns that projection into an integer count.

claims.status_anchor: Toy source-action constructions supply the missing integer count
claims.statement: Four tested toy source-action constructions do not supply the missing integer count.
A vectorlike carrier Dirac mass is allowed in the tested model, and neither tested mass branch derives three.
The candidate novelty is only the search-bounded carrier-specific inverse assembly; primary decomposition and CRT are prior art.
scope_exits: novelty of CRT or Krein transversality

claims.status_anchor: The literal generation integer on GU's 14-manifold
claims.statement: A literal integer generation observable on the true GU 14-manifold remains open.
claims.codomain: Z-valued index or rank not yet constructed
scope_exits: claim that the integer equals three

claims.statement: The reconstructed Pati-Salam branch normalizes one Spin(10) 16 as one family unit; it does not count how many families occur.
The bridge from a 3-primary torsion component to the integer three is open and is ill-typed as an additive homomorphism.
claims.codomain: missing map into a Z-valued index or rank
claims.grade: open with formal obstruction to the naive map
scope_exits: identifying a torsion representative with an integer

claims.statement: The paper does not prove that external backgrounds are necessary.

claims.status_anchor: GU forces exactly three chiral generations
claims.statement: The paper does not claim that GU forces exactly three chiral generations.
